course_title: Promoting Health through Physical Activity, Exercise and Diet

learning_outcomes.description: Define health promotion and its role in wider public health domain
Discuss current national health priorities as they relate to physical activity, exercise and nutrition
Discuss different approaches to health promotion including the advantages and disadvantages of individual and population based approaches
Critique behaviour change and socio-ecological theory and the role of environments in promoting physical activity, exercise and nutrition
Critically understand the emerging role apps, wearables and social media play in the promotion of physical activity, exercise and nutrition.
Discuss the processes used to design evidence based health promotion interventions
Discuss methods and approaches used to evaluate health promotion programs
Demonstrate the ability to effectively collaborate with others to complete group tasks

assessments.weighting: <dd>20% 10% for written critique and 10% for social media post </dd>
assessments.due_date:
28/08/2026 5:00 pm

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia, Written
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Essay/ Critique, Presentation
assessments.task_description:
You will be given a contemporary health promotion issue (e.g. Coca Cola is responsible for the obesity epidemic). Using a debate style format, present a critical synthesis of empirical evidence in a one-page written format along with a 2 min video on the health promotion issue.

Recording of Assessment:

Assessment Procedure

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained as per Appendix 1 as the official recording of the item for re-mark purposes.

Recordings will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed for the purposes of:

i. moderation of marking

ii. provision of feedback to the student(s) recorded; and/or

iii. re-marking following a successful re-mark application.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
